options.A: Invariant rational functions are a subset of the rational matrix which includes rational right and left null spaces as components.
options.B: Null spaces of a rational matrix influence the root set of invariant rational functions but are not part of their definition.
options.C: Null spaces of a rational matrix, including rational right and left null-spaces, are distinct and unrelated to invariant rational functions.
options.D: Invariant rational functions directly determine the structural composition of the rational matrix, unrelated to null spaces.

explanation: Invariant rational functions are part of the rational matrix, which includes components such as the rational right and left null spaces, Nr(R(s)) and Nℓ(R(s)), respectively. These null spaces are directly related to the rational matrix and thus indirectly related to the invariant rational functions, making option A the best description of their relationship within the context provided.
